ip_outcome_note: PokemonTCG.io API confirms card name 'Cubchoo', National Pokedex no. 613, Water-type, Unova region (Gen 5). Vision pass describes pale blue bear cub with dripping nose and large expressive eyes in snowy environment — matches canonical Cubchoo appearance. IP is Game Freak/Nintendo-internal.

trivia_bullets.text: Darkness Ablaze (SWSH3) released August 14, 2020. Cubchoo (National Pokedex no. 613) appears as a Water-type common at no. 48/189. The Water-type classification in the TCG maps to Cubchoo's Ice-type in the mainline games — Ice-types are consistently printed as Water-type in the TCG [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-48].

Cubchoo (National Pokedex no. 613) is an Ice-type introduced in Pokemon Black & White (Gen 5, Unova, 2010). It evolves into Beartic starting at level 37. The design was created by a Game Freak staff member who had been wanting to use an idea involving freezing snot for some time; the mucus mechanic is the central design hook [WebSearch: bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Cubchoo_(Pokemon); WebSearch: pokemaniacal.com 'Cubchoo and Beartic'].

Cubchoo's nasal mucus is both its defining visual trait and a built-in health indicator mechanic: when healthy, the snot is thick and sticky and Ice-type techniques are stronger. When unhealthy, the mucus becomes watery or disappears and Ice moves weaken. The dripping blue snot visible in the card art is thus a sign of good health [WebSearch: bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Cubchoo_(Pokemon)].

"When this Pokemon is in good health, its snot becomes thicker and stickier. It will smear its snot on anyone it doesn't like." — card flavor text [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-48]. The flavor text reframes what looks like a hygiene problem as a health signal and a form of social enforcement — the snot is both diagnostic and weaponized.
